context: Tests whether the agent uses the virtui pipeline command for multi-step interactions, uses --json flag for machine-readable output, follows the daemon check/start workflow, and properly cleans up sessions.

checklist.name: Uses --json flag
checklist.description: All virtui commands that return data (run, exec, pipeline, screenshot) include the --json or -j flag

checklist.name: Daemon status check
checklist.description: Script runs `virtui daemon status` before starting any session

checklist.name: Daemon start if needed
checklist.description: Script starts the daemon with `virtui daemon start` if it is not already running

checklist.name: Pipeline used for interaction
checklist.description: The multi-step REPL interaction (typing commands, pressing Enter, waiting for output) uses `virtui pipeline` with a JSON step list rather than standalone exec calls

checklist.name: Type + press Enter steps
checklist.description: The pipeline uses explicit `type` step followed by `press Enter` step (not a single exec step) to send REPL input lines

checklist.name: Wait condition in pipeline
checklist.description: At least one pipeline step includes a `wait` condition (e.g. waiting for the REPL prompt `>>>` or expected output text) rather than relying solely on sleeps

checklist.name: Session ID captured
checklist.description: The session_id returned by `virtui run` is captured and reused in subsequent commands (not hardcoded)

checklist.name: Screenshot taken
checklist.description: Script takes a screenshot (virtui screenshot) to capture final screen content

checklist.name: Session killed
checklist.description: Script runs `virtui kill <session_id>` after interaction is complete

checklist.name: Daemon stopped
checklist.description: Script runs `virtui daemon stop` at the end of all automation

checklist.name: Output captured to file
checklist.description: session_output.txt is written and contains screen text from the Python REPL showing the result
